← Ontology

Regenerative_Agriculture

Parent item
33314415-10e1-8101-a15c-f3113a0d0ee2
long_description
Farming and grazing systems turn on whether soil, water, and biodiversity are treated as the foundation of production or as collateral damage. This tag marks the shift toward practices that can feed communities and ecosystems over long horizons—agroforestry, holistic grazing, and field systems where yield and living systems are not assumed to be enemies.
slug
Regenerative_Agriculture
Activities
33314415-10e1-8155-965a-e79041c09b81, 33314415-10e1-81db-8e4f-dadc911e47a7, 33314415-10e1-814b-b2b3-cfd941f548c8, 33314415-10e1-813c-b738-c4869386f6bf, 33314415-10e1-81e1-a7e3-c17751e3828e, 33314415-10e1-815d-8ae3-c660f727b67e, 33314415-10e1-8190-b896-e293d6563720, 33314415-10e1-81f3-b0e6-fcc0d77bccf3, 33314415-10e1-814f-a6c8-cca8058b7b45, 33314415-10e1-81dd-9707-f60321ea6276, 33314415-10e1-814d-9e49-f66ef5f746b4, 33314415-10e1-81d6-a35a-c245e17b72d3, 33314415-10e1-81ba-b45e-d630344839b5, 33314415-10e1-8107-a169-c5a716220df0, 33314415-10e1-814d-a3d5-d72f435ad4ca, 33314415-10e1-8148-a973-f67bb18c1dd3
Description
Initiatives that shift farming, grazing, and agroforestry systems toward soil health, biodiversity, water retention, and long‑term community nourishment.
parent_pattern_name
Agro-Bioeconomy
